tools.name: usage_guide
tools.description:
Instructions for correctly using this MCP server.
        Call at the start of a session to learn tools, auth, CLI prerequisites, and stash file handling.

tools.name: agents_create
tools.description:
Create a Camber agent.

        Creates a personal agent by default. Pass `team_name` to create the agent
        for a team you belong to. Returns the created agent, including its `tag`.

tools.name: user_info
tools.description:
Get the authenticated Camber user and team names.

        Returns email, username, and teams list.

tools.name: agents_chat_start
tools.description:
Start a Camber agent chat. This is the tool to use for chatting with an agent.

        Agent runs can take minutes — longer than MCP tool timeouts allow (Claude Desktop
        cannot extend them). So this tool does NOT wait for the reply: it submits the
        message and returns immediately with

tools.name: agents_chat_status
tools.description:
Check an async agent chat started with agents_chat_start, and return its reply when done.

        This checks the CLI twice over 30 seconds and returns its latest status response.
        Status values:
        - `running`: still working — call this tool again with the same conversation_id.

tools.name: agents_list
tools.description:
List Camber agents with optional scope and pagination.

        Returns: AgentListInfo object containing:
        - data: List of AgentInfo objects
        - page: PageInfo object with pagination details (page, size, total, num_page)

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "agents_chat_start" is 1439 chars, far longer than this server's median (210). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Start a Camber agent chat. This is the tool to use for chatting with an agent. Agent runs can take minutes — longer than MCP tool timeouts allow (Claude Desktop…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "agents_chat_start" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Start a Camber agent chat. This is the tool to use for chatting with an agent. Agent runs can take minutes — longer than MCP tool timeouts allow (Claude Desktop…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: user_info, agents_chat_start, agents_chat_status · Private data: usage_guide, agents_chat_status · Public sink: user_info, agents_chat_start · Destructive: agents_chat_start, agents_chat_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: user_info, agents_chat_start, agents_chat_status · Private data: usage_guide, agents_chat_status · Public sink: user_info, agents_chat_start · Destructive: agents_chat_start, agents_chat_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
